sections.subtitle: Team Overview and League Standings
sections.text: Girona FC, currently sitting in 16th place in the league with 38 points, are set to host Villarreal, who are in a much stronger 5th position with 58 points. This matchup at Estadi Montilivi in Girona presents a challenging scenario for the home side. Girona will be looking to climb out of the lower half of the table. Meanwhile, Villarreal aim to solidify their impressive standing. The disparity in points—20 between the two—highlights the uphill battle Girona faces.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context and Recent Head-to-Head
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Villarreal holds the upper hand with 3 wins compared to Girona’s 1, with 1 draw. Their most recent encounter on December 1, 2024, ended in a thrilling 2-2 draw, with Thierno Barry earning Player of the Match honors with an 8.1 Sofascore rating. Head-to-head streaks show Villarreal often being the first to score in 8 out of 8 matches. Girona has struggled to keep a clean sheet against Villarreal in their last 8 meetings. Additionally, matches between these teams often see more than 4.5 cards in 5 out of 6 games.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Girona FC
sections.text: For Girona, Yangel Herrera stands out with a league rank of 3 for times fouled per game at 2.3, alongside 4 goals and 3 assists this season. Veteran forward Cristhian Stuani leads the attack with 9 goals, overperforming his expected goals of 6.96 by 2.04. Midfielder Viktor Tsygankov also contributes significantly with 2 goals and 4 assists, boasting a solid 7.09 average rating. However, Girona will miss forward Abel Ruíz, who has 4 goals this season, due to absence. Defender Daley Blind, with a 7.07 rating and 2 assists, will be crucial in organizing the defense.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Villarreal
sections.text: Villarreal boasts some top-ranked talent, including Ayoze Pérez, who ranks 4th in the league for goals with 16, significantly overperforming his expected goals of 11.16 by 4.84. Despite the absence of Alejandro Baena, ranked 3rd for assists with 8 and 1st for key passes with 88, Villarreal’s attack remains potent. Baena’s absence is a blow, as is that of Gerard Moreno, ranked 3rd for aerial duels won at 80%. However, Nicolas Pépé, in form with a 7.6 average rating over the last 5 matches, and Yéremy Pino with 5 assists, will look to exploit Girona’s defense.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Playing Styles
sections.text: Girona FC excels in possession, ranking 3rd in the league with an average of 57.26%, and also in accurate passes at 87.88%. They are strong in the final third, ranking 3rd for successful final third passes per game at 102.85. However, defensively, they struggle, conceding 52 goals and ranking 17th for least goals conceded. Villarreal, on the other hand, has scored heavily in key periods, with 14 goals between minutes 31-45, representing 17.02% of their total. Both teams create chances, with Girona ranking 12th for big chances created at 63 and Villarreal likely to capitalize given their recent form.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics and Referee Impact
sections.text: The referee for this clash, Isidro Diaz de Mera Escuderos, averages 5.9 yellow cards per game and 0.31 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter. Girona commits many fouls, ranking 17th with 383 fouls this season, and averages 2.12 yellow cards per game. Matches involving both teams often see less than 10.5 corners in 4 out of 5 head-to-heads, aligning with Girona’s average of 5.12 corners per game, ranking 5th in the league. Expect a game with physical challenges and frequent stoppages due to fouls.

sections.subtitle: Scoring Patterns and Game Trends
sections.text: Girona scores most of their goals late, with 25% of their 41 goals coming between minutes 76-90. Villarreal also peaks late, with 19.15% of their goals in the same period, alongside strong output between minutes 31-45. Recent streaks show Girona involved in games where both teams score in 8 out of 10 matches. Villarreal’s games often see more than 2.5 goals in 4 out of 5 recent matches. Girona also tends to concede first in 7 out of 9 games, which could set the tone early.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Absences Impact
sections.text: Girona will miss forward Abel Ruíz and midfielder Bryan Gil, both key contributors with combined 7 goals and 3 assists. Villarreal faces significant absences too, with Alejandro Baena and Gerard Moreno out, impacting their creative and aerial threat. These absences could balance the game somewhat, though Villarreal’s depth with players like Ayoze Pérez and Nicolas Pépé might still give them an edge. Girona must capitalize on home advantage at Estadi Montilivi to compensate for their missing players.
